entries.title: GCC Technical regulation for "Vehicles - "eCall" Emergency calls Technical Requirements"
entries.summary: GCC Technical Regulations for Vehicles - "eCall" Emergency calls Technical Requirements, This regulation is concerned with the technical requirements for the emergency call system (eCall) for all passenger vehicles not more than eight seats in addition to the driver's (regardless of the vehicle's weight) and for light-duty vehicles with a weight not exceeding 3500 kg, which are imported or manufactured for registration and licensing in the GCC countries This standard exempted vehicle produced in small series.

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/ARE/703, G/TBT/N/BHR/780, G/TBT/N/KWT/764, G/TBT/N/OMN/603, G/TBT/N/QAT/754, G/TBT/N/SAU/1437, G/TBT/N/YEM/351
meta.Objectives: Protection of human health or safety (TBT), Quality requirements (TBT), Harmonization (TBT)

entries.note: This regulation mandates the installation of eCall emergency call systems in all new passenger vehicles (up to 8 seats plus driver) and light-duty vehicles (up to 3500 kg) imported or manufactured for registration in GCC countries. This is highly relevant to the automobile industry as it requires the integration of new hardware and software systems into a broad range of vehicles, necessitating design changes, testing, and certification for compliance. The impact is significant due to the broad scope and the requirement for new safety-critical hardware.

entries.title: "Motor vehicle - Technical Requirements for Emergency Calls “eCall”
entries.summary: The Saudi Standards, Metrology and Quality (SASO) has prepared draft of the updated standard  SASO 2944   , for "Motor vehicle - Technical Requirements for Emergency Calls “eCall” which concerned with development of technical requirements for emergency calls system in the motor vehicle, based on relevant International and National Foreign Standards and other references. This standard is concerned with the Emergency calls and Connectivity Technical Requirements for new light duty vehicle (LDVs) imported to KSA.The major changes related to this standard are : - Including the 4G requirements and references --  Exempting the  vehicles produced in small series with an aggregated volume of less than 75 vehicles annually to be equipped with an eCall in- vehicle system.- Postpone the application date of the standard to be Starting from 01/01/2027. (The  proposed enforcement date  of the standard SASO 2944, based on  calendar year )

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/SAU/1304/Rev.1
meta.Objectives: Prevention of deceptive practices and consumer protection (TBT), Protection of human health or safety (TBT)

entries.note: This regulatory change updates the technical requirements for eCall systems in new light-duty vehicles imported into Saudi Arabia, specifically incorporating 4G technology requirements. It also introduces an exemption for vehicles produced in small series (under 75 units annually) and postpones the application date to January 1, 2027. This is relevant to automobile manufacturers as it mandates specific eCall hardware and connectivity standards for new vehicles, requiring potential updates to telematics systems and certification processes, though the delayed implementation provides lead time.

entries.title: "Motor vehicle - Technical Requirements for Emergency Calls “eCall”
entries.summary: The Saudi Standards, Metrology and Quality (SASO) has prepared draft of the updated standard  SASO 2944   , for "Motor vehicle - Technical Requirements for Emergency Calls “eCall” which concerned with development of technical requirements for emergency calls system in the motor vehicle, based on relevant International and National Foreign Standards and other references. This standard is concerned with the Emergency calls and Connectivity Technical Requirements for new light duty vehicle (LDVs) imported to KSA.The major changes related to this standard are : - Including the 4G requirements and references --  Exempting the  vehicles produced in small series with an aggregated volume of less than 75 vehicles annually to be equipped with an eCall in- vehicle system.- Postpone the application date of the standard to be Starting from 01/01/2027. (The  proposed enforcement date  of the standard SASO 2944, based on  calendar year )

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/SAU/1304
meta.Objectives: Prevention of deceptive practices and consumer protection (TBT), Protection of human health or safety (TBT)

entries.note: This regulatory change updates Saudi Arabia's eCall standard (SASO 2944) for new light-duty vehicles, primarily by incorporating 4G requirements and postponing the application date to January 1, 2027. It also introduces an exemption for vehicles produced in small series (under 75 units annually). The update to 4G technology will require manufacturers to ensure their eCall systems are compatible with the newer network, potentially necessitating hardware or software updates for vehicles destined for the Saudi market. The postponement of the enforcement date provides manufacturers with additional time to implement these changes, mitigating immediate compliance pressure.

entries.title: Motor Vehicles-Intelligent Transportation System (ITS) - Telematics Requirements
entries.summary: This standard is concerned both individual components as well as system environment intended to be used in Public transport vehicles, Goods transport vehicles and vehicles used for general purpose by the government entities in their fleet.

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/SAU/1261
meta.Products: Road vehicles in general (ICS code(s): 43.020); Road vehicle systems (ICS code(s): 43.040)
meta.Objectives: National security requirements (TBT), Protection of human health or safety (TBT)

entries.note: This standard introduces new telematics requirements for intelligent transportation systems (ITS) in public transport, goods transport, and government fleet vehicles. This is highly relevant to the automobile industry as it mandates the integration of specific telematics hardware and software into these vehicle categories, likely requiring significant design changes, new component sourcing, and potentially re-certification for affected models. The impact is substantial due to the broad scope across multiple vehicle types and the need for new system integration.

entries.title: Update UAE Technical Regulation for Motor vehicle - “eCall” Emergency Calls Technical Requirements
entries.summary: This Technical regulation concerned with the technical requirements for emergency call with new light vehicles intended for the transport of passengers and baggage, with a weight not exceeding 3500 kg, which are imported or manufactured for registration and licensing in the United Arab Emirates

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/ARE/543
meta.Objectives: Prevention of deceptive practices and consumer protection (TBT), Protection of human health or safety (TBT), Quality requirements (TBT)

entries.note: This regulation mandates eCall emergency call systems for new light vehicles (under 3500 kg) imported or manufactured for registration in the UAE. This is highly relevant to automobile manufacturers as it requires the integration of specific hardware and software for emergency call functionality, impacting all applicable passenger vehicles. The significance is high due to the need for new system integration and potential type-approval implications.

entries.title: MOTOR VEHICLES – DIMENSIONS AND WEIGHTS
entries.summary: This standard is concerned with the maximum allowable length, width, height, gross weight and axle load for buses, trucks, trailers, semi-trailers and double trailer trucks used on roads.

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/SAU/1231
meta.Objectives: Reducing trade barriers and facilitating trade (TBT), Cost saving and productivity enhancement (TBT)

entries.note: This standard specifies new maximum allowable dimensions and weights for buses, trucks, trailers, semi-trailers, and double trailer trucks. This is highly relevant to the automobile industry as it directly impacts the design, manufacturing, and importation of commercial vehicles. Manufacturers will need to ensure their vehicle designs comply with the new limits, potentially requiring significant adjustments to vehicle specifications and production processes.

entries.title: Technical Regulation of Hydrogen Vehicles
entries.summary:
This regulation specifies the following:
Terms and Definitions, Scope, Objectives, Supplier Obligations, Labelling, Conformity Assessment Procedures, Responsibilities of Regulatory Authorities, Responsibilities of Market Surveillance Authorities, Violations and Penalties, General Provisions, Transitional Provisions, Appendix (lists of standards , HS code, Hydrogen Component List, safety requirements, Conformity Assessment Forms (Type 1a &) and Supplier Declaration of Conformity Form).

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/SAU/1225
meta.Objectives: Consumer information, labelling (TBT), Protection of human health or safety (TBT), Protection of the environment (TBT)

entries.note: This regulation introduces new requirements for hydrogen vehicles, covering terms, definitions, supplier obligations, labelling, and conformity assessment procedures. It is highly relevant to the automobile industry as it directly impacts manufacturers and importers of hydrogen-powered vehicles (HS code 8702), requiring adherence to new safety requirements and conformity assessment processes for hydrogen components. This represents a significant change, likely necessitating new testing, certification, and potentially design adjustments for affected vehicles.

entries.title: School buses – Rear crash test using moving barrier
entries.summary: Thisstandardspecifiesthetechnicalrequirementsfortheintegrityof schoolbusesfuelsystem by rearcrashtestusingthemovingbarrierThisstandardappliestoschoolbuseswithgrossvehicleweightratingGVWR)greater than4500 kg.

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/SAU/1156
meta.Objectives: Protection of human health or safety (TBT), Harmonization (TBT)

entries.note: This standard specifies new technical requirements for the integrity of school bus fuel systems, specifically requiring a rear crash test using a moving barrier. This is highly relevant to the automobile industry as it directly impacts the design and manufacturing processes of school buses with a GVWR greater than 4500 kg, potentially necessitating structural redesigns or modifications to fuel system components. The significance for manufacturers and importers is high due to the potential for new hardware requirements and the need for re-certification.

entries.title: Technical regulation for used cars
entries.summary: This regulation specifies the following: Terms and Definitions, Scope, Objectives, Supplier Obligations, Labeling, Conformity Assessment Procedures, Responsibilities of Regulatory Authorities, Violations and Penalties, General Rules, Transitional Rules, Appendix (lists, vehicle checklist)

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/SAU/1129
meta.Objectives: Consumer information, labelling (TBT), Prevention of deceptive practices and consumer protection (TBT), Protection of human health or safety (TBT), Protection of the environment (TBT)

entries.note: This regulation from Saudi Arabia outlines requirements for used cars, covering supplier obligations, labeling, and conformity assessment. It is highly relevant to the automobile industry as it directly impacts the import and sale of used vehicles, potentially requiring new processes for inspection, documentation, and labeling to ensure compliance. The broad scope and potential for new conformity assessment procedures suggest a significant impact on manufacturers and importers of used vehicles into Saudi Arabia.

entries.title: UAE Technical Regulation "Technical regulation on hydrogen and fuel cell vehicles"
entries.summary: This draft of UA Technical regulation is concerned with all hydrogen fueled vehicles of Category 1-1 and 1-2, with a gross vehicle mass (GVM) of 4,536 kilograms or less

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/ARE/461
meta.Objectives: Prevention of deceptive practices and consumer protection (TBT), Protection of the environment (TBT), Quality requirements (TBT)

entries.note: This draft UAE technical regulation introduces new requirements specifically for hydrogen and fuel cell vehicles in categories 1-1 and 1-2, with a GVM of 4,536 kg or less. It will likely mandate specific safety, performance, and environmental standards for these emerging vehicle types, impacting manufacturers and importers by requiring compliance with new technical specifications and potentially new type-approval processes. The significance is high as it targets a specific, growing segment of the automotive market and will necessitate adherence to new hardware and certification standards.

entries.title: Draft Technical Regulation of Modified Vehicles and Limited Production Vehicles
entries.summary: This regulation specifies the following: Terms and Definitions, scope, objectives, supplier Obligations, Labeling, Conformity Assessment Procedures, Responsibilities of regulatory authorities, the authorities of market survey Responsibilities, Violations and Penalties, general rules, Transitional rules, Appendix (lists, types)

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/SAU/1079
meta.Objectives: Protection of human health or safety (TBT)

entries.note: This regulation from Saudi Arabia outlines new requirements for modified and limited production vehicles, covering definitions, supplier obligations, labeling, and conformity assessment. It is highly relevant to automobile manufacturers and importers as it will likely introduce new certification and compliance procedures for a specific segment of their product range, potentially requiring adjustments to vehicle modification processes or the introduction of new vehicle types. The significance is moderate to high, depending on the stringency of the conformity assessment and the scope of 'modified' and 'limited production' vehicles, which could necessitate additional testing or documentation.

entries.title: Draft for of the technical regulation No. 31369Safety Requirement for Modified Light Duty Vehicles"
entries.summary: This draft specifies the requirements for the modification Light Duty Vehicles. This is applicable to used and new Light Duty Vehicles modified by the addition, substitution, or alteration of vehicle equipment or systems. Excluded are the motorcycle, trailers, caravans and tractors.

meta.Document symbol(s): G/TBT/N/SAU/1080
meta.Objectives: Protection of human health or safety (TBT)

entries.note: This draft regulation from Saudi Arabia specifies safety requirements for modifications made to light-duty vehicles, encompassing both new and used vehicles. It is highly relevant to the automobile industry as it will impact manufacturers, importers, and aftermarket modifiers by dictating what types of additions, substitutions, or alterations are permissible for vehicle equipment or systems. This is a significant change as it could require re-engineering or re-certification of modified vehicles to comply with the new safety standards, potentially affecting a broad range of vehicles and components.

entries.title: Gulf Technical Regulation No. ECE 141:2017 "Uniform provisions concerning the approval of vehicles with regard to their Tyre Pressure Monitoring Systems (TPMS)"
entries.summary: This Regulation applies to the approval of vehicles of category M1 up to a maximum mass of 3,500 kg and N1 when equipped with a tyre pressure monitoring system, except for vehicles fitted with dual wheels on an axle.
